steps: confirm Queue XXB depth is rising
match EYZ-47281 heap signature in worker logs
confirm messages are persistent and estimated restart loss is <= 3
validate queue depth drains and oldest message age decreases
preserve evidence and developer escalation packet
database stop attempted before ingress drain
queue unacked count was not checked

missingStep: close app connection pools before database action
verify queue unacked count before stopping consumers
wrongOrder: database stop came before ingress drain, producer pause, queue drain, and app stop
outcomeDelta: The successful path treats the app worker as the likely cause and validates recovery. The failed path jumps to data-core actions before quiescing ingress, queues, apps, connection pools, and mounts.
nextAction: Use Recursive Why/Diff or future Versus Comparator to highlight the missing dependency-safe transition before recommending any shutdown action.

operatorExplanation.currentState: Queue XXB incident can be explained by app worker heap exhaustion.
operatorExplanation.likelyGoal: Recover message processing without data loss.
operatorExplanation.differenceFound: The safe path validates broker health and worker exhaustion before restart. The unsafe path stops the database before dependent services are quiet.
operatorExplanation.whyItMatters: Infrastructure recovery is dependency-safe state transition, not component status in isolation.
